matrix.case: single mutation increments version exactly once (not zero, not two)
N sequential mutations increment monotonically by exactly 1 each
per-node isolation: mutating node A does not bump node B's version
concurrent mutations under real Lock/Unlock discipline produce exactly the expected total version (no lost updates, no double counts), -race clean
no regression to existing single-threaded btree behavior (encode/decode, insert incl. splits, delete incl. merges, lookup, prefix scan)
matrix.covered_by: full engine/btree/... -race suite, unchanged, all passing
